metadata.description: Validation rubrics per track. Each rubric defines criteria, proficiency levels and observable indicators aligned with module exit_criteria in 42_lausanne_curriculum.json.
metadata.levels_note: Levels are progressive: novice → apprentice → competent → proficient. A learner should reach 'competent' before moving to the next module and 'proficient' before considering the track complete.

levels.label: Novice
levels.description: Recognizes concepts but cannot apply them independently. Needs step-by-step guidance.

levels.label: Apprentice
levels.description: Applies concepts with prompting or reference material. Makes mistakes but can self-correct with hints.

levels.label: Competent
levels.description: Applies concepts independently in familiar situations. Can explain reasoning and catch own errors.

levels.label: Proficient
levels.description: Applies concepts fluently in new situations. Can teach others and reason about trade-offs.

criteria.label: Command mastery
criteria.description: Fluency with core filesystem commands (pwd, ls, cd, mkdir, touch, cp, mv, rm).

indicators.novice: Knows command names but confuses flags. Needs to look up basic usage each time.
indicators.apprentice: Uses commands correctly with common flags. Occasionally confuses cp/mv or forgets rm -r for directories.
indicators.competent: Navigates, creates and cleans file trees without hesitation. Uses man to resolve uncommon flags.
indicators.proficient: Combines commands fluently in one-liners. Understands edge cases (hidden files, symlinks, glob patterns).

criteria.label: Pipeline fluency
criteria.description: Ability to compose redirections, pipes and filters into useful command chains.

indicators.novice: Understands that | connects commands but cannot build a working pipeline.
indicators.apprentice: Builds simple 2-command pipelines (e.g. cat | grep). Confuses > and >>.
indicators.competent: Builds 3+ stage pipelines. Redirects stderr independently. Uses grep, sort, uniq, wc fluently.
indicators.proficient: Designs pipelines for real tasks (log analysis, data extraction). Understands when to use tee, xargs or process substitution.

criteria.label: Permissions understanding
criteria.description: Ability to read, set and diagnose file permissions and execution rights.

indicators.novice: Knows chmod exists but cannot read rwx triads or octal notation.
indicators.apprentice: Can set basic permissions (chmod +x, chmod 755) but struggles with user/group/other distinctions.
indicators.competent: Reads ls -l output fluently. Diagnoses permission denied errors. Understands PATH for script execution.
indicators.proficient: Understands setuid, sticky bit, umask. Can explain security implications of permission choices.

criteria.label: Script autonomy
criteria.description: Ability to work daily in a Linux terminal using find, man, vim and git without GUI dependency.

indicators.novice: Relies on web search for every question. Cannot open or edit a file in vim.
indicators.apprentice: Uses man for common commands. Can open, edit and save in vim. Runs basic git commands with reference.
indicators.competent: Answers questions using man without web search. Edits confidently in vim. Uses git for personal versioning.
indicators.proficient: Uses find with complex predicates. Navigates man efficiently. Has a repeatable git workflow with meaningful commits.

criteria.label: Syntax and control flow
criteria.description: Ability to write clean, warning-free C code with proper structure.

indicators.novice: Can write a main() that compiles but ignores warnings. Confuses declaration and assignment.
indicators.apprentice: Uses if/else and loops correctly. Writes functions but forgets prototypes or header guards.
indicators.competent: Compiles with -Wall -Wextra -Werror and zero warnings. Splits code across files with proper headers.
indicators.proficient: Writes norminette-clean code on first pass. Chooses appropriate types and control structures for the problem.

criteria.label: Pointer mastery
criteria.description: Understanding of addresses, pointers, arrays and string manipulation through memory.

indicators.novice: Knows pointers exist but cannot dereference or pass by reference correctly.
indicators.apprentice: Uses pointers for simple pass-by-reference. Confuses array indexing and pointer arithmetic.
indicators.competent: Manipulates strings via pointers. Allocates and frees memory without leaks. Draws memory diagrams.
indicators.proficient: Understands pointer-to-pointer, function pointers. Recognizes and prevents dangling pointers, double free, buffer overflow.

criteria.label: Memory safety
criteria.description: Discipline around dynamic allocation, leak prevention and valgrind usage.

indicators.novice: Uses malloc but never frees. Does not know valgrind exists.
indicators.apprentice: Frees allocated memory in simple cases. Runs valgrind but cannot interpret all errors.
indicators.competent: Achieves valgrind-clean runs consistently. Frees in all code paths including error handling.
indicators.proficient: Designs allocation strategies (who owns, who frees). Prevents leaks by construction, not just testing.

criteria.label: Build fluency
criteria.description: Mastery of compilation, Makefiles and systematic debugging with gdb and valgrind.

indicators.novice: Compiles with cc main.c and hopes for the best. Does not use a Makefile.
indicators.apprentice: Writes a basic Makefile. Uses -Wall but ignores some warnings. Debugs by adding printf.
indicators.competent: Makefile with all/clean/fclean/re. Uses gdb to find segfaults. Follows a systematic debug workflow.
indicators.proficient: Makefile handles dependencies correctly. Uses gdb watchpoints and conditional breakpoints. Combines gdb + valgrind efficiently.

criteria.label: Library and algorithm design
criteria.description: Ability to build reusable functions, maintain API consistency and reason about complexity.

indicators.novice: Writes ad-hoc functions with inconsistent naming. No test strategy.
indicators.apprentice: Implements basic utility functions (strlen, strcpy). Tests manually with a main.
indicators.competent: Builds 5+ libft-style functions with consistent prototypes. Writes a test harness. Knows O(n) vs O(n^2).
indicators.proficient: Designs a coherent function library. Reasons about when to optimize. Tests edge cases systematically.

criteria.label: Python fundamentals
criteria.description: Fluency with types, control flow, functions and data structures.

indicators.novice: Can print and assign variables. Writes loops but confuses list and dict syntax.
indicators.apprentice: Uses if/for/while correctly. Writes functions with parameters. Uses lists and dicts for simple tasks.
indicators.competent: Uses list comprehensions. Chooses the right data structure. Writes scripts that read files and process data.
indicators.proficient: Writes idiomatic Python. Uses generators, unpacking, built-in functions fluently. Solves algorithmic problems cleanly.

criteria.label: OOP clarity
criteria.description: Ability to design classes, handle files and build command-line tools.

indicators.novice: Knows the class keyword but cannot write __init__ or use self correctly.
indicators.apprentice: Defines classes with attributes and methods. Reads JSON but struggles with nested structures.
indicators.competent: Designs class hierarchies. Builds CLI tools with argparse. Handles errors with try/except.
indicators.proficient: Applies OOP principles (encapsulation, composition over inheritance). Structures projects with modules and packages.

criteria.label: Scripting autonomy
criteria.description: Ability to build useful automation scripts that handle real-world input.

indicators.novice: Writes scripts that work for hardcoded input only.
indicators.apprentice: Handles file I/O and basic command-line arguments. Scripts break on unexpected input.
indicators.competent: Builds robust scripts with error handling, file validation and structured output.
indicators.proficient: Writes production-quality scripts with logging, configuration and clear separation of concerns.

criteria.label: AI integration readiness
criteria.description: Understanding of RAG, prompt design, source governance and the boundary between AI assistance and dependency.

indicators.novice: Uses AI tools as a black box. Cannot explain what RAG means or why sources matter.
indicators.apprentice: Understands retrieve-then-generate. Writes basic prompts but cannot evaluate response quality.
indicators.competent: Builds a minimal RAG pipeline. Evaluates AI responses against sources. Explains source-governance tiers.
indicators.proficient: Designs prompts with constraints. Detects hallucinations. Applies source policy in practice. Knows when not to use AI.
